prose:
Fukui electrophilic descriptor (f+) maximum for acrolein = 0.64232102 (dimensionless).

Method provenance
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui indices (f+, f−, dual) for acrolein; headline number is the largest f+ value (atom 4, carbonyl C).
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’) with exact four-center integrals (no RI density-fitting) in PySCF 2.x.
• Geometry provenance: fixed XYZ supplied by user (/home/erobles/…/acrolein.xyz); no further optimization (single-point population analysis).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no continuum model).
• Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (neutral singlet). N ± 1 states were treated as doublets (charges ±1, multiplicity 2) following default rules.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`, PySCF backend). Command recorded in result JSON (see cli_invocation).
• Convergence: SCF cycles for neutral, cation, and anion all converged; integrity.trustworthy = true.

- fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
- schema_header: common header present
